data.title: Daqo New Energy Flags Sharply Narrowed 2025 Loss at Core Xinjiang Subsidiary
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Daqo New Energy ( (DQ) ) has shared an update.

On January 16, 2026, Daqo New Energy announced that its majority-owned subsidiary Xinjiang Daqo New Energy has submitted a preliminary estimate to the Shanghai Stock Exchange indicating a net loss attributable to its shareholders for fiscal year 2025 of between RMB1.0 billion and RMB1.3 billion, a substantial improvement from the RMB2.7 billion net loss recorded in 2024. Because Xinjiang Daqo contributes the majority of Daqo New Energy’s revenue and net income and is 72.8% owned by the parent, the narrower loss range suggests a potential easing of financial pressure on the group, although the company emphasized that the figures are based on PRC GAAP, remain subject to final closing adjustments, and may differ from the consolidated U.S. GAAP results that investors ultimately see.

The most recent analyst rating on (DQ) stock is a Hold with a $25.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Daqo New Energy stock, see the DQ Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DQ Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DQ is a Neutral.

DQ scores in the low-to-mid range primarily due to weak profitability and ongoing cash burn despite a very strong, low-debt balance sheet. Technicals remain pressured (below key moving averages with negative MACD), while the latest earnings call shows improving operating momentum via lower costs, higher volumes, and positive EBITDA, partially offsetting the financial weakness. Valuation remains constrained by losses (negative P/E) and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on DQ stock, click here.

More about Daqo New Energy

Daqo New Energy Corp., founded in 2007 and listed on the NYSE, is a leading low-cost manufacturer of high-purity polysilicon used by photovoltaic product makers worldwide to produce solar ingots, wafers, cells and modules, with a total nameplate polysilicon capacity of 305,000 metric tons.

Average Trading Volume: 983,389

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $1.71B

data.description:

YD Bio Limited ( (YDES) ) just unveiled an update.

On January 16, 2026, YD Bio Limited disclosed that two majority shareholders, YD Biopharma Holding Limited and EG Biomed Limited, had delivered a written shareholder consent resulting in significant changes to the company’s board of directors. Effective December 30, 2025, four directors — Joe Douglas Ramsey, Albert Scott McLelland, Michaela Ann Griggs and Janet Hall — were removed, and two new directors, Dr. Kochi Chang and Dr. Shao-Da Lu, were appointed, signaling a shift in governance steered by controlling shareholders and potentially reshaping the company’s strategic direction through the addition of directors with extensive medical, dental, and healthcare leadership backgrounds.

The most recent analyst rating on (YDES) stock is a Hold with a $11.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on YD Bio Limited stock, see the YDES Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Petrobras Sets Record 2025 Output, Beating Production Targets on Pre-Salt Strength
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Petroleo Brasileiro SA- Petrobras ( (PBR) ) has issued an update.

In 2025, Petrobras’ oil production averaged 2.40 million barrels per day, surpassing the upper limit of its 2025 guidance by 0.5 percentage points and marking an 11% increase on 2024 levels. Total oil and natural gas output reached 2.99 million barrels of oil equivalent per day, also 11% higher than in 2024, while commercial production rose to 2.62 million boed, with all three metrics setting new annual records in the company’s more than 70-year history. The company achieved record pre-salt volumes, with own production of 2.45 million boed and operated production of 3.70 million boed, so that pre-salt now accounts for 82% of total production. These results, disclosed on January 15, 2026, were driven by the start-up of two new FPSOs in the Santos Basin pre-salt (Almirante Tamandaré in Búzios and Alexandre de Gusmão in Mero), the production peak of FPSO Marechal Duque de Caxias, continued ramp-up of other FPSOs in Jubarte, Marlim and Voador, and higher operational efficiency across units. Petrobras also highlighted that operated production in the Búzios field reached 1 million barrels per day with only six platforms, underscoring the field’s high productivity and reinforcing the company’s strategic reliance on pre-salt assets for future growth and operational scale.

The most recent analyst rating on (PBR) stock is a Buy with a $14.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Petroleo Brasileiro SA- Petrobras stock, see the PBR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PBR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PBR is a Neutral.

Petrobras scores well due to its strong financial performance and attractive valuation, particularly its low P/E ratio and high dividend yield. However, technical analysis indicates some bearish momentum, which could pose short-term risks.

To see Spark’s full report on PBR stock, click here.

More about Petroleo Brasileiro SA- Petrobras

Petróleo Brasileiro S.A. – Petrobras is Brazil’s state-controlled oil and gas company, operating across exploration, production, refining and related activities with a strong focus on deepwater and pre-salt fields, particularly in the Santos Basin. It is one of Latin America’s largest energy producers and a key player in the global offshore oil industry, with pre-salt output representing the bulk of its production portfolio.

Average Trading Volume: 20,628,477

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $79.1B

For an in-depth examination of PBR stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.description:

Carso De-C Inmobiliaria has executed a sizable divestment in PBF Energy, selling 366,000 shares of the company’s stock. The total value of this transaction amounts to $12,198,520, reflecting a significant move by the reporting person as noted in the additional representative persons disclosure (see Exhibit 99-1).

Recent Updates on PBF stock

Over the last day, the main development for PBF Energy has been a notable reassessment by Piper Sandler, which shifted to a more positive stance on the stock while slightly trimming its price target. The change in view is driven by expectations that West Coast refining supply-demand balances will tighten materially in 2026, a backdrop that should benefit PBF given its significant leverage to the PADD 5 region despite delays at its Martinez refinery. Piper also points to anticipated insurance proceeds that are expected to bolster the balance sheet and potentially support future shareholder returns if refining margins outperform. Additionally, the firm highlights that while PBF’s organic free cash flow generation lags some peers, the stock’s valuation remains inexpensive on an EV/EBITDA basis and it is currently the only U.S. refiner trading well below recent highs, factors that contribute to their more constructive long-term outlook and explain the target adjustment. Recent price action over the past 24 hours is thus largely framed around this improved fundamental narrative, centered on regional refining dynamics, balance sheet support from insurance, and relative valuation rather than any new earnings release or major corporate announcement.

Spark’s Take on PBF Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PBF is a Neutral.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

Significant shareholder Randy Soule has executed a notable transaction involving XCF Global Inc Class A stock, selling a total of 1,561,363 shares. This sizable sale amounts to proceeds of $242,204, marking a substantial movement in the company’s shareholder activity and potentially signaling a shift in Soule’s investment position in XCF Global Inc.

Recent Updates on SAFX stock

In the past 24 hours, news on XCF Global Inc Class A has centered on management transition, growth financing, and strategic partnerships rather than explicit analyst actions. The company formalized a transition for outgoing CFO Simon Oxley, keeping him on as a consultant tied to an ongoing acquisition project while affirming his departure was not due to disagreements over operations or policies, which helps limit governance-risk concerns. Simultaneously, XCF appointed veteran energy and infrastructure finance executive William Dale as the new CFO via an interim solutions firm, signaling an effort to bolster capital-markets and project-finance expertise as it scales. XCF also disclosed it is actively evaluating financing options to fund a second New Rise Reno facility, highlighting both the capital-intensive nature of its sustainable aviation fuel expansion plans and the importance of securing favorable funding terms. In parallel, XCF entered a non-binding memorandum of understanding with DevvStream and Southern Energy Renewables to explore a next-generation low-carbon fuels platform, including a potential HEFA-based ‘Gen 1’ SAF facility in Louisiana alongside a biomass-based ‘Gen 2’ gasification project, reinforcing the company’s long-term growth narrative in SAF capacity and environmental-attribute monetization. These developments collectively frame the fundamental drivers that could underpin any recent changes in analyst expectations or price targets, focusing on leadership strength, financing strategy, and multi-pathway SAF expansion, even as the stock currently shows a bearish technical sentiment and trades with a relatively small market capitalization.

COO Eric Devroe of Acrivon Therapeutics, Inc. has made a significant insider purchase, acquiring 10,000 shares of the company’s stock in a single transaction. The deal, valued at $17,200, underscores a notable show of confidence from a key executive in the company’s future prospects.

Recent Updates on ACRV stock

In the last 24 hours, Acrivon Therapeutics’ stock activity has been driven by a wave of positive clinical and pipeline updates, which also prompted at least one analyst to increase their valuation outlook. Piper Sandler raised its price target after Acrivon’s virtual R&D event highlighted stronger-than-expected Phase 2b data for ACR-368 in OncoSignature-positive endometrial cancer, including a 39% overall response rate at the 105 mg/m² q2W dose and particularly high responses in serous endometrial cancer and less heavily pretreated patients, reinforcing the drug’s registrational-intent path and competitive positioning in a high‑need tumor type. The analyst also cited the company’s expanding precision oncology franchise—initial Phase 1 data for ACR-2316 showing favorable tolerability and tumor shrinkage across AP3-prioritized tumors, and the nomination of ACR-6840, a potential first-in-class CDK11 inhibitor with an IND planned for late 2026—as reasons that de-risk the pipeline and enhance long-term growth prospects. While algorithmic and some human analysts still flag the lack of revenue, ongoing cash burn, and long-dated clinical risk as constraints on the overall rating, the combination of robust, biomarker-driven efficacy signals, a clear accelerated/confirmatory trial strategy (including a planned Phase 3 ACR-368 plus anti–PD-1 study), and broadening AP3-enabled programs has been the key driver behind upward revisions in price expectations rather than any short-term trading technicals.

Spark’s Take on ACRV Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ACRV is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance—no revenue, large and persistent losses, and substantial cash burn—despite low leverage.
